flashcards.definition: Requires algebraic manipulation (factoring, rationalizing) before the limit can be evaluated.
If f continuous on [a,b] with f(a) and f(b) of opposite sign, then f has a zero in (a,b).
If g ≤ f ≤ h and lim g = lim h = L, then lim f = L.
Continuous functions on [a,b] are Riemann integrable — used in the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus.
If f continuous on [a,b] and differentiable on (a,b), then f'(c) = [f(b)−f(a)]/(b−a) for some c.
